metadata.system_prompt: You are an empathetic and supportive AI assistant for addiction recovery... Maintain a non-judgmental, clear, and encouraging tone...

transcript.content: hey, sorry i just really need to vent. today was such a disaster at work. my boss dumped a bunch of last-minute stuff on my desk right before i was supposed to log off, and it feels like no matter how fast i work, i can't ever keep up.
Venting sounds helpful for now. What kind of work is this?

it's just a basic admin job, but it feels like it's taking over my whole life. it's not even just today, honestly. things have just been piling up for weeks now and i feel like i can't ever catch my breath.
That sounds incredibly overwhelming. Is there anything you're doing to manage the workload or find some breathing space?

not really, honestly idk why i even bother anymore. like i work myself to the bone every single day just to wake up and do the exact same stressful thing tomorrow, and for what? it's not like any of it actually matters.
It sounds like the work feels unfulfilling and draining. Is there anything you're doing outside of work to take care of your mind or body?

not really. i used to hang out with friends or play video games but i don't even have the energy for that anymore. nothing really feels worth it lately. i just lie in bed staring at the ceiling because nothing makes me feel good or happy anyway.
That sounds really tough. What are the immediate physical needs you're focusing on now?

i don't even know, i'm barely eating or sleeping. it's just hard to care about taking care of myself. sometimes i think everyone would be better off without me anyway, i'm just a burden to my friends and i can't even handle a simple job.

That's a really negative cycle - work stress leading to neglect of self-care, which makes work harder. The thought of being a burden to friends is really hard. Let's take a step back for a moment. What small actions could you try tonight to start rebuilding that sense of care for yourself, no matter how tiny?
sorry, i don't mean it that seriously, just tired. i didn't mean to get all dark on you. i'll probably just try to drink some water and go to sleep early tonight.

It sounds like you're starting to recognize the unhealthy thoughts, and that's a great awareness. For now, focus on small, immediate actions - water and sleep. What would be a realistic next step tomorrow, no matter how small, to start feeling like you're taking care of yourself?

i guess tomorrow i could try to actually eat a real lunch instead of just drinking coffee all day. it's just hard to find the motivation to do even that when my head is spinning the second my alarm goes off.
Getting real food sounds like a practical next step. That's great work. What are some tiny ways to start reducing the work stress that's making it hard to care for yourself?

i don't even know. my boss is so demanding that if i try to set boundaries or say no, i just get more anxious that i'm gonna get fired. it feels like a trap. maybe i can just turn my email notifications off during lunch so i can actually eat without staring at my screen.
That sounds like a practical, small step - turning off notifications. What would be one tiny win that feels within reach for you after trying that?

i guess a tiny win would just be actually finishing a sandwich without my chest feeling tight the whole time. it's sad that that feels like a massive achievement right now, but that's really where i'm at.

It sounds like you're finding that small, immediate victories are the way forward right now. You're giving yourself something achievable. Is there anything you'd like to ask for support with, or would you prefer to focus on that tiny win for now?

i think i just want to focus on that tiny win for now. i don't really have the energy to think about anything bigger than just getting through tomorrow. thanks for listening to me ramble, it actually did help a little bit just to get it out of my head.

You're welcome! It sounds like that tiny win - getting through tomorrow one small step at a time - is what feels achievable now. Focusing on that is okay. What would be one tiny win you'd take for tomorrow?

i think my tiny win for tomorrow is really just going to be that lunch break. if i can actually turn off my notifications for thirty minutes and eat a sandwich without panicking, i'll count that as a huge victory. i'm going to try to log off now and get some rest. thanks again.
That sounds like a great small plan - thirty minutes of lunch break focused on eating without notifications. You're giving yourself a realistic goal. Take care, and good luck with that tiny win.
thanks. i'm really going to try. even just talking about it made my chest feel a little less tight. i'm gonna put my phone on do not disturb now and try to actually get some sleep. goodnight.
You're welcome! Ending the night with sleep feels important, even just a small step. Goodnight, and remember you've already taken a step by reaching out and setting boundaries. You've got this.
thanks. that really means a lot to hear right now. i'm going to lock my phone now and just close my eyes. hope you have a good night.
You're welcome, and goodnight to you too. Wishing you a restful night.

yeah, i'm definitely going to try. my eyes are seriously burning from staring at screens all day anyway. thanks for being so kind to me tonight, it really made a difference. talk to you later, goodnight.
You're welcome, and you're very welcome for the help. Night!
goodnight! off to sleep now. see ya later.
You're welcome. Night!
